zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir John William Nicholas Carter of Cusworth於1450年出生在英國賀特福爾沃特福德時，他的父親William Carter31歲，母親Elizabeth Convert22歲。他於大約1470年與Lady Margaret Frazier在英國越可爾多卡斯特結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1500年6月在他的家鄉享年50歲，埋葬在英國賀特福爾沃特福德。

nameMeanings.name: Carter
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a transporter of goods, from Middle English carter(e) ‘carter’ (Anglo-Norman French car(e)tier, Old French charetier, medieval Latin carettarius, carettator). The Old French word coalesced with the earlier Middle English word cart(e) ‘cart’, which is from either Old Norse kartr or Old English cræt, both of which, like the Late Latin word, were probably derived from Celtic. This surname is also very common among African Americans.
Irish: shortened form of   McCarter  .
Americanized form of German   Karter   ‘carder’.

variants.name: Charman
meanings: English (Sussex and Surrey): occupational name for a carter, carrier, or coachman, from Middle English (Old French) char, car ‘car, cart, carriage’ + Middle English man ‘man’. Compare Carter and Carman 2.

English (Sussex and Surrey): occupational name for someone who did odd jobs, from Middle English char(re), chere (Old English cier(r), cer(r), cear(r), cær(r)) ‘turn (of activity), small job, chore’ + man. Compare Carman 2.
In some cases probably also an Americanized form of Slovenian Čarman (see Carman 5).

variants.name: Chartier
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Armand, Normand, Jacques, Marcel, Adelard, Alcide, Emile, Gilles, Raoul, Rosaire, Alphonse.
French: shortened form of charretier, an occupational name for a carter, from an agent derivative of Old French charette ‘cart’.
French Canadian: altered form of English   Carter  , a cognate of 1 above. Compare   Sharkey   and   Shorkey  .

variants.name: Karter
meanings: German: occupational name for a carder (someone who prepares wool for spinning), from Middle High German karter. Compare Carter .

variants.name: McCarter
meanings: Scottish and northern Irish: variant of   McArthur  .

variants.name: Carrier
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Andre, Marcel, Emile, Lucien, Cecile, Alain, Gaetan, Gilles, Jacques, Laurent, Normand.

English and southern French: from Middle English, Old French car(r)ier (from Late Latin carrarius, a derivative of carrum ‘cart, wagon’, a word of Gaulish origin), an occupational name for a carter (see Carter ), in French also for a cartwright. Compare French Charrier .
French: occupational name for a stonemason or quarryman, carrier. Compare Coyer .

variants.name: Cartman
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Lancashire): occupational name from Middle English cartman ‘man who operated a cart for the transport of goods’. Compare Carter .
Americanized form of German Kartmann (see Kartman ).

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament: ‘God is gracious’. See John .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

nameMeanings.name: Nicholas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the post-classical Greek personal name Nikolaos, derived from nikē ‘victory’ + laos ‘people’. The spelling with -ch- first occurred as early as the 12th century, and became firmly established at the time of the Reformation, although Nicolas is still occasionally found. St Nicholas was a 4th-century bishop of Myra in Lycia, about whom virtually nothing factual is known, although a vast body of legend grew up around him, and he became the patron saint of Greece and of Russia, as well as of children, sailors, merchants, and pawnbrokers. His feast day is 6 December , and among the many roles which legend has assigned to him is that of bringer of Christmas presents, in the guise of ‘Santa Claus’ (an alteration of the Dutch form of his name, Sinterklaas).

variants.name: Nickolas
meanings: Mainly U.S.: variant spelling of   Nicholas  , influenced by the short form   Nick .

variants.name: Nicolas
meanings: From Late Greek. See Nicholas .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir John William Nicholas Carter of Cusworth was born in 1450, in Watford, Hertfordshire, England, his father, William Carter, was 31 and his mother, Elizabeth Convert, was 22. He married Lady Margaret Frazier about 1470, in Doncaster, Yorkshire, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. He died in June 1500, in his hometown, at the age of 50, and was buried in Watford, Hertfordshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir John William Nicholas Carter of Cusworth est né en 1450, à Watford, Hertfordshire, Angleterre, son père, William Carter, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Convert, avait 22 ans. Il a épousé Lady Margaret Frazier environ 1470, à Doncaster, Everwyck, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé en juin 1500, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 50 ans, et a été enterré à Watford, Hertfordshire, Angleterre.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir John William Nicholas Carter of Cusworthが1450年にイギリスハートフォードシュアイアワトフォードで生まれた時，父William Carterは31歳，母Elizabeth Convertは22歳であった。約1470年にイギリスヨークシュアイアドンカスターでLady Margaret Frazierと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1500年6月に故郷で50歳で死去し，イギリスハートフォードシュアイアワトフォードにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir John William Nicholas Carter of Cusworth(이)가 1450년 영국 허트퍼드실르 왓퍼드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William Carter(은)는 31세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth Convert(은)는 22세였다. 그는 대략 1470년 영국 요르크실르 돈카스터에서 Lady Margaret Frazier(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1500년 6월 그의 고향에서 50세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 허트퍼드실르 왓퍼드에 묻혔다.

Ele casou-se com Lady Margaret Frazier aproximadamente 1470, em Doncaster, Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 2 filhas. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir John William Nicholas Carter of Cusworth родился в 1450 г. в местности ватфорд, хиртфордшир, Англия, его отцу по имени William Carter было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Convert, 22. Он женился на женщине по имени Lady Margaret Frazier около 1470 г. в местности донкастир, еркшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер июнь 1500 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 50 лет и был похоронен в местности ватфорд, хиртфордшир, Англия.
